AddDatasetExamples.name: AddDatasetExamples
AddDatasetExamples.documentation:
Adds examples to the dataset's DRAFT.

Validation: All examples are validated against the dataset's schemaType before any writes occur. If any example fails validation, the entire batch is rejected with ValidationException — no examples are written (all-or-nothing semantics).

Asynchronous: Operates in-place on DRAFT. No version bump occurs. Use CreateDatasetVersion to publish DRAFT as a new numbered version.

State guard: Returns ConflictException (DATASET_NOT_READY) if the dataset status is not in {DRAFT, ACTIVE}.

Request size limit: Max 5 MB total request body. Max 1000 examples per call.

CreateDataset.name: CreateDataset
CreateDataset.documentation:
Creates a new Dataset resource asynchronously.

Returns immediately with status CREATING. Poll GetDataset until status transitions to ACTIVE or CREATE_FAILED (with failureReason).

CreateDatasetVersion.name: CreateDatasetVersion
CreateDatasetVersion.documentation:
Publishes the current DRAFT as a new numbered version.

Snapshots the DRAFT examples as the next version (0→1, 1→2, ...). The DRAFT is preserved and remains editable after publishing. Returns immediately with status PUBLISHING. Poll GetDataset until status transitions to ACTIVE or PUBLISH_FAILED.

State guard: Returns ConflictException (DATASET_NOT_READY) if the dataset status is not in {DRAFT, ACTIVE, PUBLISH_FAILED}.

Quota: MAX_VERSIONS_PER_DATASET applies to published versions only (not DRAFT).

DeleteDataset.name: DeleteDataset
DeleteDataset.documentation:
Deletes a dataset version or an entire dataset (all versions + name claim). Asynchronous 202.

State transitions:

  • If datasetVersion is absent (full delete): status transitions to DELETING immediately.
  • If datasetVersion is provided (version-specific delete): status transitions to UPDATING.

State guard (full delete): Returns ConflictException (DATASET_NOT_READY) if the dataset status is in {CREATING, UPDATING}. Deletion is allowed from ACTIVE, CREATE_FAILED, UPDATE_FAILED, and DELETE_FAILED states.

State guard (version-specific delete): Returns ConflictException (DATASET_NOT_READY) if the dataset status is not in {ACTIVE, CREATE_FAILED, UPDATE_FAILED}.

Fails with ConflictException (REFERENCED_BY_EVAL_JOB) if referenced by an active evaluation job (full delete only).

If the delete workflow fails after retries, status is set to DELETE_FAILED (full delete) or UPDATE_FAILED (version-specific delete). Calling DeleteDataset on a DELETE_FAILED dataset re-triggers the delete workflow (idempotent retry path).

Version parameter:

  • If datasetVersion is absent: deletes ALL versions and the Dataset record itself.
  • If datasetVersion is provided: deletes only that specific DatasetVersion. Returns ResourceNotFoundException if the specified version does not exist.

DeleteDatasetExamples.name: DeleteDatasetExamples
DeleteDatasetExamples.documentation:
Deletes specific examples by ID from DRAFT.

Validation: All example IDs are validated before any deletes occur. If any ID does not exist in DRAFT, the entire batch is rejected with ResourceNotFoundException — no examples are deleted (all-or-nothing semantics).

Asynchronous: Operates in-place on DRAFT. No version bump occurs. Use CreateDatasetVersion to publish DRAFT as a new numbered version.

State guard: Returns ConflictException (DATASET_NOT_READY) if the dataset status is not in {DRAFT, ACTIVE}.

GetDataset.name: GetDataset
GetDataset.documentation:
Retrieves dataset metadata only.

Use ?datasetVersion=DRAFT or ?datasetVersion=N to retrieve a specific version's metadata. If absent, defaults to DRAFT (the latest unpublished version). Returns ResourceNotFoundException if the specified version is not found.

State guard: Returns ConflictException (DELETION_IN_PROGRESS) if the dataset status is DELETING — the resource exists but is mid-deletion and should not be read.

For paginated example IDs use ListDatasetExamples.

ListDatasetExamples.name: ListDatasetExamples
ListDatasetExamples.documentation:
Returns paginated example IDs from the dataset.

Version-pinned pagination: The server embeds the resolved version in the nextToken. Once pagination begins, all subsequent pages are pinned to that version regardless of concurrent mutations or whether datasetVersion is passed on subsequent requests. The datasetVersion query parameter is only used for the first request (when nextToken is absent); if omitted, defaults to DRAFT.

State guard: Returns ConflictException (DELETION_IN_PROGRESS) if the dataset status is DELETING.

ListDatasets.name: ListDatasets
ListDatasets.documentation: Lists all datasets in the caller's account, paginated. No presigned URLs in list results.

UpdateDataset.name: UpdateDataset
UpdateDataset.documentation:
Updates a dataset's metadata. Synchronous operation. Only provided fields are updated; omitted fields remain unchanged.

To modify dataset content, use AddDatasetExamples, UpdateDatasetExamples, or DeleteDatasetExamples.

Cannot update: name, schemaType, kmsKeyArn (immutable after creation).

UpdateDatasetExamples.name: UpdateDatasetExamples
UpdateDatasetExamples.documentation:
Updates multiple existing examples in-place on DRAFT.

Validation: All examples are validated against the dataset's schemaType before any writes occur. If any example fails validation, the entire batch is rejected with ValidationException — no examples are updated (all-or-nothing semantics).

Asynchronous: Operates in-place on DRAFT. No version bump occurs. Use CreateDatasetVersion to publish DRAFT as a new numbered version.

Fails with ResourceNotFoundException if any exampleId does not exist in DRAFT. To add new examples, use AddDatasetExamples instead.

State guard: Returns ConflictException (DATASET_NOT_READY) if the dataset status is not in {DRAFT, ACTIVE}.

Request size limit: Max 5 MB total request body. Max 1000 examples per call.

examples.documentation: Examples to add. Each example is assigned an auto-generated UUID. Max 1000 examples per call. Total request body must not exceed 5 MB.

datasetName.documentation: Human-readable name for the dataset. Unique within the account (case-insensitive). Immutable after creation.

schemaType.documentation: Versioned schema type governing the structure of examples. Immutable after creation.

kmsKeyArn.documentation: Optional AWS KMS key ARN for SSE-KMS on service S3 writes.

status.documentation: Always CREATING immediately after this call. Poll GetDataset until ACTIVE or CREATE_FAILED.
Always PUBLISHING immediately after this call. Poll GetDataset until ACTIVE or PUBLISH_FAILED.

content.documentation: Validated against dataset.schemaType by SchemaRegistry at write time. Stored as-is.

DatasetExample.documentation:
A single example in a dataset.

content is a free-form JSON Document whose structure is defined by the dataset's schemaType. The service validates content against the declared schema at write time using SchemaRegistry. Content is stored as-is — no normalization is applied.

exampleId.documentation: Must exist in DRAFT; fails with ResourceNotFoundException if not found.

content.documentation: New content for the example. Validated against dataset.schemaType at write time.

DatasetExampleUpdate.documentation: An example update entry containing the exampleId and new content.

s3.documentation: Customer S3 URI. Service reads using the caller's FAS credentials.

inlineContent.documentation: Raw file bytes embedded in the request body. Maximum 5 MB decoded. For larger files, upload to S3 and use the s3 union member.

DatasetSource.documentation:
Source of the dataset file. Either a customer S3 URI or inline bytes.

Inline content is limited to 5 MB (enforced by @length constraint on the content blob). For files larger than 5 MB, use the s3 union member with a pre-uploaded S3 URI.

DatasetStatus.documentation:
Dataset lifecycle status.

State transition rules: CreateDataset: CREATING → DRAFT | CREATE_FAILED CreateDatasetVersion: DRAFT | ACTIVE | PUBLISH_FAILED → PUBLISHING → ACTIVE | PUBLISH_FAILED AddDatasetExamples / UpdateDatasetExamples / DeleteDatasetExamples: (asynchronous, in-place on DRAFT — no status change) Allowed when status in {DRAFT, ACTIVE} Error state if operation fails: UPDATE_FAILED DeleteDataset (full): DELETING → (record deleted) | DELETE_FAILED

Operation guards: GetDataset / ListDatasetExamples: ConflictException(DELETION_IN_PROGRESS) if status == DELETING CreateDatasetVersion: ConflictException(DATASET_NOT_READY) if status not in {DRAFT, ACTIVE, PUBLISH_FAILED} AddDatasetExamples / UpdateDatasetExamples / DeleteDatasetExamples: ConflictException(DATASET_NOT_READY) if status not in {DRAFT, ACTIVE} UpdateDataset: ConflictException(DATASET_NOT_READY) if status not in {DRAFT, ACTIVE, CREATE_FAILED, UPDATE_FAILED, PUBLISH_FAILED} DeleteDataset: ConflictException(DATASET_NOT_READY) if status in {CREATING, PUBLISHING}

DatasetVersion.documentation: Dataset version. Accepts "DRAFT" or a version number.

datasetVersion.documentation: Optional version to delete. Use "DRAFT" or omit to delete the draft. Returns ResourceNotFoundException if the specified version does not exist.

Document.documentation: Free-form JSON document. Used for dataset example content whose structure is governed by the dataset's schemaType.

datasetVersion.documentation: Version to retrieve: "DRAFT" or a version number. Defaults to DRAFT if absent.

failureReason.documentation: Populated when status is CREATE_FAILED, UPDATE_FAILED, PUBLISH_FAILED, or DELETE_FAILED.

source.documentation: Indicates how the dataset was originally provided. For S3-sourced datasets, contains the S3 URI. For inline-created datasets, this field is omitted.

schemaType.documentation: The schema type declared at create time. Immutable after creation.

kmsKeyArn.documentation: AWS KMS key ARN used for SSE-KMS on service S3 writes, if configured.

content.documentation: Maximum 5 MB (5,242,880 bytes). Use S3 source for larger files.

KmsKeyArn.documentation: Supports both standard single-region keys (UUID format) and multi-region keys (mrk- prefix).

datasetVersion.documentation: Version to paginate: "DRAFT" or a version number. Defaults to DRAFT if absent. Only used on the first request (when nextToken is absent). For subsequent pages, the version is extracted from the nextToken and this parameter is ignored.

exampleIds.documentation: List of example IDs in this page.

s3Uri.documentation: S3 URI in format s3://bucket-name/path/to/file. Path must be URL-encoded if it contains special characters.

SchemaType.documentation:
Format of a customer-provided source file.

JSONL (JSON Lines, one object per line) is the service's native ingestion and storage format — it can be streamed line-by-line without loading the entire file into memory, which is important as the row-count cap is extended.

JSON array ([{...},{...}]) is intentionally not supported at launch: it cannot be streamed (requires loading the full file into memory to parse), and all major eval frameworks (LangSmith, Ragas, DeepEval, Arize Phoenix) export as JSONL or CSV — not JSON arrays. Customers with JSON array files can Versioned schema type for dataset examples.

Each value identifies both the source format and the version of that format's schema. Schema definitions (required/optional fields) are stored as constants in SchemaRegistry. The schemaType on a Dataset is immutable after creation.

When a framework changes its format, a new version is added (e.g., RAGAS_V2) without breaking existing datasets using the old version. Content is always stored as-is.

examples.documentation: Examples to update. Each entry must include an existing exampleId and new content. Max 1000 examples per call. Total request body must not exceed 5 MB.
